title: At the pharmacy
setting: You have a headache and an upset stomach. You walk into a small farmacia.

steps.speaker: Farmacéutica
steps.en: Hello. How can I help you?

options.feedback: Perfect. 'Me duele' + body part is exactly how you describe pain.
True and useful, but vague. Telling her what hurts gets you the right medicine faster.
This is a pharmacy, not a comedor! Tell her what's wrong.

steps.speaker: Farmacéutica
steps.en: And does your stomach hurt too?

options.feedback: Simple and clear. 'Un poco' is a handy way to soften an answer.
Fine — though a word or two more helps her help you.
Honest, and a perfectly good thing to say when stuck! She'll rephrase.

steps.speaker: Farmacéutica
steps.es: Le doy estas pastillas. Una cada ocho horas.
steps.en: I'll give you these pills. One every eight hours.

options.feedback: Always good to ask the price. Clear and practical.
A fine clarifying question if you missed it — never be afraid to ask.
You came in sick — turning down the medicine leaves you no better off!
Lovely. 'Muy amable' is a warm, very Nica way to thank someone.
Forty córdobas for medicine is fair — and you don't haggle at a pharmacy!
